data.description:
## Problem
The `supply-chain` code-review dimension is a pure-LLM overlay (`registry.py` OVERLAY_IDS) with
NO deterministic backing — there is no pip-audit / OSV / hash-pin / registry lookup anywhere in
the code-review path. Package existence and provenance are live-registry, deterministic questions
the model cannot answer from a diff. In the field it asserted a real package published one day
after the model's training cutoff "does not correspond to any real published release," citing its
own absence-of-knowledge (`evidence: ABSENCE`) as proof of fabrication. All 7 flagged packages
were genuine. The failure is systematic (fires on any post-cutoff release, i.e. exactly on
lockfile churn) and inverts trust (legitimate packages labelled fabricated).

## Idea
Introduce a DETERMINISTIC supply-chain detector (analogous to the existing `detectors.py`
deterministic detectors) that verifies lockfile entries — name / version / hashes / pins —
against the configured package index, and/or shells out to pip-audit / OSV for the advisory
(known-vulnerability) dimension. The deterministic result is AUTHORITATIVE for existence and
provenance. The LLM overlay then DROPS the existence/nonexistence claim entirely and only judges
what it can see in the diff (e.g. "a new dependency was added; check license/scope"), never
asserting a package is fabricated.

## Why deterministic beats LLM here
Existence/provenance is a registry fact, not a reasoning judgement. A registry query is correct
and cheap; an LLM is structurally blind to anything after its cutoff. This removes an entire
false-positive class the model cannot fix on its own.

## Open questions / design notes
- Network policy: gates may run offline/attested. Needs an offline fallback ("not checked" is the
  honest output when no index is reachable) — never fabricate a verdict either direction.
- Where to slot it: extend `run_detectors` vs a new detector module; how the deterministic verdict
  merges with (and overrides) the LLM overlay's finding.
- Probably warrants a short ADR (introduces a network-touching detector + fallback contract).

## Provenance
Discovered from the upstream review-code false-positive report (client project). Sibling of the
merge-base diff fix (ticket 8559-44c3-e08a-4bb9) and the pass-2 temperature change.
data.title: Deterministic supply-chain assessment for code review (replace LLM existence/provenance claims)
